item.title: Buffalo Bayou Cruise: Bats
item.description:
Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 7:20 PM

Location: ALLEN’S LANDING, 1001 Commerce Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Sail the scenic waterway on a 90-minute, guided tour from downtown to Waugh Drive Bridge and back to watch the emergence of the 250,000+ Mexican free-tailed bats that live under the bridge. Our knowledgeable guide will share details about the bats’ eating patterns, behavior and contributions to the bayou’s ecosystem. Space is limited, and boat tours fill up fast. Boat departs 45 minutes before sunset. Cost Starting at $30/person Duration 90 minutes Departure location Allen’s Landing (1019 Commerce St.) Please note, children must be at least four years old to board the pontoon boat.   PURCHASE TICKETS

item.title: Bat Chat
item.description:
Friday, June 19, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Waugh Drive Bat Bridge, 1

Stroll to Waugh Drive Bridge for a Bat Chat with the Houston Area Bat Team and see the bat colony up close! Did you know? These population of Mexican Free-tailed Bats at the Waugh Bridge can reach a peak of approximately 300,000 in the summer months! Around sunset, the colony leaves the crevices beneath the bridge and create a whirlwind-like formation as they emerge. Join the Houston Area Bat Team to learn and discover as they share fun, educational facts and insights about these miniature, flying mammals! Invite your family and friends, bring a picnic blanket, wear your bug spray, and admire this unique phenomena. Bat Chats are held every Friday evening from May to August as part of our Summer Species series, starting 30 minutes before sunset.   Cost Free Time 7:30 – 8:30pm (30 minutes before sunset) Meeting Location Waugh Bridge Bat Colony Observation Deck (along Allen Parkway) REGISTRATION

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Sound Healing Meditation
item.description:
Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: BUFFALO BAYOU PARK CISTERN, 105 Sabine Street, Houston, TX, 77007, United States

Step into a deeper stillness inside the spacious and tranquil Buffalo Bayou Park Cistern. Offered in collaboration with UNIONisCREATION™, this meditation and sound experience invites guests into a space of resonance, restoration, and inner alignment. Through gentle vibrations of singing bowls, wind chimes, and a gong, along with guided awareness, participants are supported in reconnecting body and mind. This series is more than meditation – it is a practice of listening, feeling, and returning to center within one of Houston’s most iconic, immersive spaces. Each session unfolds as a shared moment of reflection, connection, and sound-guided stillness. Guests should bring their own cushions or mats. No food or drinks are allowed inside the Cistern. Cost: $20 PURCHASE TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Buffalo Bayou Cruise: History
item.description:
Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: ALLEN’S LANDING, 1001 Commerce Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Look back at Houston’s history while cruising Buffalo Bayou on a 90-minute tour with a local historian. Your guide will share stories of the Allen brothers and provide historical information about the people, places, and events along the bayou that helped shape Houston.   Cost Adults: $40 Children: $32 (ages 4-12)   Departure Location Allen’s Landing (1019 Commerce St.) Weather permitting. Reservations Required. Children under the age of 4 are not permitted on the pontoon boat.   PURCHASE TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Bikes & Bats
item.description:
Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: THE WATER WORKS, 105 Sabine Street, Houston, TX, 77007, United States

Join Buffalo Bayou Partnership and BikeHouston for a batty good time with a bike ride to the Waugh Bridge Bat Colony! Bring your bike for an evening ride along Buffalo Bayou to the Waugh Drive Bridge, where you’ll witness an unforgettable sight—more than 300,000 Mexican free-tailed bats emerging at sunset. Along the way, bat expert DeAndra Ramsey will share insights about these fascinating and important creatures—affectionately known as “sky puppies.” Spots are limited, so reserve yours today! Cost $10 Duration 2 hours / 3 miles Meeting Location The Water Works in Buffalo Bayou Park (105-B Sabine St) TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

item.description:
Sunday, June 21, 2026 at 5 PM

Wednesday, June 24, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Step into a deeper stillness inside the spacious and tranquil Buffalo Bayou Park Cistern. Join Buffalo Bayou Partnership on a free Wellness Walk led by Laura Conely, founder of Urban Paths. Event details

item.title: Bat Chat
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Waugh Drive Bat Bridge, 1

Stroll to Waugh Drive Bridge for a Bat Chat with the Houston Area Bat Team and see the bat colony up close! Did you know? These population of Mexican Free-tailed Bats at the Waugh Bridge can reach a peak of approximately 300,000 in the summer months! Around sunset, the colony leaves the crevices beneath the bridge and create a whirlwind-like formation as they emerge. Join the Houston Area Bat Team to learn and discover as they share fun, educational facts and insights about these miniature, flying mammals! Invite your family and friends, bring a picnic blanket, wear your bug spray, and admire this unique phenomena. Bat Chats are held every Friday evening from May to August as part of our Summer Species series, starting 30 minutes before sunset.   Cost Free Time 7:30 – 8:30pm (30 minutes before sunset) Meeting Location Waugh Bridge Bat Colony Observation Deck (along Allen Parkway) FREE REGISTRATION

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

item.title: The Marvelous Miss Gender starring BOSCO - Ages 18+ w/ Valid ID
item.description:
Wednesday, July 22, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: House of Blues Houston, 1204 Caroline Street, Houston, TX

Murray & Peter Present: "The Marvelous Miss Gender" starring BOSCO. From RuPaul's Drag Race Season 14, 2022 and most recently All Stars 10 from 2025, BOSCO is now headlining her first ever solo tour. Meet & Greet add-on tickets are sold separately. To attend the M&G, fans must also purchase a ticket to the event. Show starts at 8pm. There will be a 20-minute intermission. Ages 18+ welcome w/ Valid ID for entry. Check DragFans.com and HereIsBosco.com for tour info VIP Tables seat 4 humans and are first come first serve if you purchase in that section. Please arrive early so that our hosts may seat your party together. Murray & Peter Present: "The Marvelous Miss Gender" starring BOSCO. From RuPaul's Drag Race Season 14, 2022 and most recently All Stars 10 from 2025, BOSCO is now headlining her first ever solo tour. Meet & Greet add-on tickets are sold separately. To attend the M&G, fans must also purchase a ticket to the event. Show starts at 8pm. There will be a 20-minute intermission. Ages 18+ welcome w/ Valid ID for entry. Check DragFans.com and HereIsBosco.com for tour info VIP Tables seat 4 humans and are first come first serve if you purchase in that section. Please arrive early so that our hosts may seat your party together. ⚠️ Duration is approximate — automated verification pending. Source: https://www.ticketmaster.com/the-marvelous-miss-gender-starring-bosco-houston-texas-07-22-2026/event/3A006469C4B6C4C8

item.title: The Music Man
item.description:
Tuesday, September 15, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: The hobby center, 800 Bagby St, Houston, TX 77002, United States, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Seventy-six trombones. One unforgettable musical! Meredith Willson’s six-time Tony Award®-winning beloved musical comedy The Music Man marches into Houston with laughter, heart, and all the music you love. Following the fast-talking traveling salesman Harold Hill as he cons the people of River City, Iowa, into buying instruments and uniforms for a boys’ band that he vows to organize. Featuring songs such as “Seventy-Six Trombones,” “Trouble,” “’Till There Was You,” “Pick-a-Little,” and “Gary, Indiana,” The Music Man is a classic musical to be shared with every generation. CRITICAL ACCLAIM FOR THE MUSIC MAN – The Telegraph “A joyous, blissful Broadway event!” – Variety “Grand and Glorious!” “…totally enchanting abso-tootin’-lutely smashing revival. It will give you new life.” PERFORMANCE SCHEDULE Tuesday, Sept 15, 7:30 p.m. – H-E-B Community Night Wednesday, Sept 16, 7:30 p.m. Thursday, Sept 17, 7:30 p.m. Friday, Sept 18, 8 p.m. Saturday, Sept 19, 2 p.m. Saturday, Sept 19, 8 p.m. Sunday, Sept 20, 2 p.m. Sunday, Sept 20, 7:30 p.m. Tuesday, Sept 22, 7:30 p.m. Wednesday, Sept 23, 7:30 p.m. Thursday, Sept 24, 7:30 p.m. Friday, Sept 25, 8 p.m. Saturday, Sept 26, 2 p.m. — Open Captioned Saturday, Sept 26, 8 p.m. Sunday, Sept 27, 2 p.m. — ASL & Audio Described SUBSCRIBE NOW TICKETING INFO Season tickets are now available. 6-show packages start at just $34 per-show. Tickets will start at $46 and will be available beginning July 13 online or by calling TUTS Guest Services at 713-558-8887. Tickets will also available in person Monday through Friday from Noon to 6 p.m. at the TUTS Box Office, located in the Hobby Center lobby. Discounted group tickets are available for parties of 10 or more.Theatre Under The Stars has a variety of accessibility options for guests.Learn more about accessible performances and other services here. Rush tickets will be available for $35 at the box office for mezzanine and gallery seating starting 2 hours prior to curtain. Questions about parking, dining or other aspects of attending a TUTS show? We’ve got answers! RUN TIME 2 hours and 30 minutes including intermission Photos Photos by Marshall Meadows VIDEOS CREATIVE TEAM Matt Lenz Director Joshua Bergasse Choreographer Ann Beyersdorfer Set Designer Santo Loquasto Original Broadway and Tour Costume Designer Ken Billington Lighting Designer Lisa Renkel Video & Projection Designer Walter Trarbach Sound Designer Roxanne De Luna Wig & Makeup Designer Robin L. McGee Costume Coordinator Kristin Stowell Music Supervisor Thomas Fosnocht Music Director/Orchestrator ARC Casting Dan Sher/Big League Productions, Inc Producer BEHIND THE SCENES ELLIOTT ANDREWS as “Harold Hill” ELIZABETH D’AIUTO as “Marian Paroo” CHARLES ANTENEN as “Tommy Djilas” PATRICK BLASHILL as “Mayor Shinn” ADDIE JAYMES as “Amaryllis” DYLAN PATTERSON as “Winthrop Paroo” MICHAEL SANTORA as “Charlie Cowell” MADISON SHEWARD as “Zaneeta Shinn” SAVANNAH STEVENSON as “Mrs. Paroo” PAUL URRIOLA as “Marcellus Washburn” EMMANUELLE ZEESMAN as “Eulalie Mackecknie Shinn” CHRISTIAN ANDREWS as “Conductor, Constable Locke, Ensemble, u/s Olin Britt” BRYCE BAYER as “Dance Ensemble Swing, Assistant Dance Captain” ANNA CHIN as “Dance Ensemble” DANNY CRUZ as “Dance Ensemble, u/s Tommy Djilas” KALISTA CURBELO as “Dance Ensemble, u/s Zaneeta Shinn” WHITNEY DANIELS as “Alma Hix, u/s Marian Paroo” ERIN DIEHL as “Dance Ensemble, Dance Captain” CRAIG FIRST as “Jacey Squires, u/s Marcellus Washburn, u/s Charlie Cowell” BRIAN DOOLITTLE GONZÁLEZ as “Ewart Dunlop, u/s Charlie Cowell, u/s Harold Hill” SOPHIE GORON as “Swing” ALEX ISHIDA as “Ethel Toffelmeir, u/s Marian Paroo” CAMERON JANSON as “Dance Ensemble” HANNAH ROSE KIDWELL as “Mrs. Squires, u/s Mrs. Paroo, u/s Eulalie Mackecknie Shinn” KEVIN KUSKA as “Swing, u/s Marcellus Washburn, u/s Mayor Shinn” ROSE MESSENGER as “Maud Dunlop, u/s Mrs. Paroo, u/s Eulalie Mackecknie Shinn” JOSHUA PIERRE MOORE as “Olin Britt, u/s Mayor Shinn” KYLIE NOELLE PATTERSON as “Child Onstage Swing – Amaryllis/Winthrop” BRANDON RANALLI as “Dance Ensemble” AIDAN RAWLINSON as “Dance Ensemble, u/s Tommy Djilas” SHAE REYNOLDS as “Gracie Shinn, Dance Ensemble” BRAYDEN SCHILLING as “Traveling Salesperson, Ensemble, u/s Harold Hill, u/s Barbershop Quartet” MARIELLE UTAYDE as “Dance Ensemble Swing, u/s Zaneeta Shinn, u/s Gracie Shinn” MATTHEW WAUTIER-RODRIGUEZ as “Oliver Hix”
